info.description: PufferPanel API interface for both the panel and daemon. Endpoints starting with /daemon or /proxy are for nodes.
info.title: PufferPanel API

get.description: Gets all nodes registered to the panel
Gets information about a single node

put.description: Updates a node with given information

get.description: Gets the secret information needed to deploy a node.
get.summary: Gets the data to deploy a node

get.description: Gets the user information of the current user

put.description: Updates the value of a panel setting

parameters.description: The new value for the setting
parameters.name: value

get.summary: Gets registered oauth2 clients under this user

parameters.description: Information for the client to create
parameters.name: client

parameters.description: Information for the client to create
parameters.name: id

get.description: Gets servers, and allowing for filtering of servers. * is a wildcard that can be used for text inputs

parameters.description: Username to filter on, default is current user if NOT admin
parameters.name: username

parameters.description: Name of server to filter on
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: Max number of results to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: What page to get back for many results
parameters.name: page

delete.description: Deletes a server from the panel

get.summary: Gets server-level OAuth2 credentials for the logged in user

post.summary: Creates server-level OAuth2 credentials for the logged in user

get.summary: Gets all users for a server

get.description: Gets the value currently being used for the specified config key

post.description: Gets all templates which can be imported from https://github.com/PufferPanel/templates
Imports the given template from our main repo

get.description: Gets all settings specific to the current user

put.description: Updates the value of a user setting

parameters.description: The new value for the setting
parameters.name: value

get.description: Gets users, and allowing for filtering of users. * is a wildcard that can be used for text inputs
Easy way to tell if the daemon is running is by using this endpoint

head.description: Gets a list of features supported by the node

get.description: Gets the given server data from an admin's view
Gets the given server logs since a certain time period

parameters.description: Only get data from after this UNIX timestamp
parameters.name: time

post.description: Runs a command in the server

get.description: Gets a file or a file list from the server

put.description: Puts a file or folder on the server

delete.description: Deletes a file from the server

parameters.description: Wait for the operation to complete
parameters.name: wait

parameters.description: Wait for the operation to complete
parameters.name: wait

parameters.description: Wait for the operation to complete
parameters.name: wait
